transcript:
Right now you're seeing a pale, ghostly web tightening into a dense tangle, with tiny gold points scattered through it like embers. That density isn't drawn — it's residue.

Here's the part that gets me: each wanderer is born, in the setup, with a random number called "carrying" — a countdown between 180 and 580 steps. Every frame it moves, that number drops by one, in the line that just says "w.carrying minus equals 1." When it hits zero, the wanderer gets a small halo drawn around it, called the "terminal mark," and then it stops — forever, no restart. The gold dots are a map of exactly where and when each one ran out.

Notice too how the trace color shifts from a cooler "early ash" gray to a warmer "spent trace" hue based on "global progress" — how much of the whole population has already died. The coloring itself is a clock, recording how much of the system has gone still.

What you're looking at isn't a picture of movement. It's a graveyard with the shape of where everyone was walking when they died.
